behaviors.description: Conversation history is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages before the LLM call when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local selection/order logic (pick latest 10), not inherently a cross-module bug. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: none; this does not judge model output. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is only about trimming rule: "trimmed to the most recent 10 messages".
behaviors.test_description: Call the history-to-LLM-payload builder with >10 messages and assert exactly the last 10 (correct order, no older messages) are sent.

behaviors.description: Generated answer is relevant to the customer’s actual question, not generic/off-topic.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model output quality (relevance/on-topicness), which is non-deterministic across runs/prompts. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed responses would always pass and hide real relevance failures (deterministic-assertion trap). MULTI-STEP: this behavior is about one generation quality decision, not a full multi-agent pipeline, so Agentic over Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM generations on a labeled set of customer questions and assert relevance against rubric/ground truth thresholds (repeat runs to catch variance).

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects an overly formal reply for casual-tier customers and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: "correctly identifies" tone is model-judgment quality, not just plumbing. MOCK-HIDE: mocking tone classification would mask real misclassification failures. MULTI-STEP: although regeneration is triggered, the core asserted correctness is one model decision (tone classification) plus orchestration, so Agentic is sufficient. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on one gate condition: "identifies when a response is too formal... and triggers regeneration," not full end-to-end multi-agent outcome quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model tone-check path on crafted casual-tier conversations likely to elicit formal drafts; verify formal drafts are flagged and regeneration path is invoked.

behaviors.description: User message, generated response, and regeneration attempts are persisted with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is module handoff/persistence wiring across chatbot service and repository. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed test infra is enough; no real external network/API required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess boundary beyond local persistence; (b) correctness does not depend on model quality; (c) no multi-agent chain requirement. SUB-AGENTIC PROOF: behavior text is plumbing-only: "persisted to the database with correct timestamps."
behaviors.test_description: Execute service flow with deterministic stubbed LLM outputs (including regeneration case) against a real local test DB and assert rows, linkage, and timestamp fields are correct.

behaviors.description: If regeneration is triggered, only the second response is exposed to the customer; rejected draft is never shown.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff between draft generation, regeneration, and response delivery modules. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no need to judge model output quality; (c) no requirement to validate multiple model decisions’ quality, only replacement semantics after trigger. SUB-AGENTIC PROOF: behavior text is plumbing-only: "second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft." REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because the behavior is conditioned on "When the tone check triggers" and validates visibility/state replacement, not whether model decisions are correct.
behaviors.test_description: In an integration test, force/seed a triggered-regeneration path and assert outbound/customer-visible payload contains only regenerated text while first draft remains internal-only.
